brief_title: ECHELON-2: A Comparison of Brentuximab Vedotin and CHP With Standard-of-care CHOP in the Treatment of Patients With CD30-positive Mature T-cell Lymphomas
official_title: A Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Phase 3 Study of Brentuximab Vedotin and CHP (A+CHP) Versus CHOP in the Frontline Treatment of Patients With CD30-positive Mature T-cell Lymphomas
brief_summary: This is a double-blind, randomized, multicenter, phase 3 clinical trial to compare the efficacy and safety of brentuximab vedotin in combination with CHP with the standard-of-care CHOP in patients with CD30-positive mature T-cell lymphomas.

interventions.name: brentuximab vedotin
interventions.description: 1.8 mg/kg every 3 weeks by IV infusion for 6-8 cycles

interventions.name: doxorubicin
interventions.description: 50 mg/m2 every 3 weeks by IV infusion for 6-8 cycles

interventions.name: prednisone
interventions.description: 100 mg on Days 1 to 5 of each 3-week cycle, orally for 6-8 cycles

interventions.name: vincristine
interventions.description: 1.4 mg/m2 (maximum 2 mg) every 3 weeks by IV infusion for 6-8 cycles

interventions.name: cyclophosphamide
interventions.description: 750 mg/m2 every 3 weeks by IV infusion for 6-8 cycles

eligibility.criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Patients with newly diagnosed, CD30-positive mature T-cell lymphomas
* Fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG)-avid disease by PET and measurable disease of at least 1.5 cm by CT
* Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status less than or equal to 2

Exclusion Criteria:

* History of another primary invasive malignancy that has not been in remission for at least 3 years
* Current diagnosis of primary cutaneous CD30-positive T-cell lymphoproliferative disorders and lymphomas or mycosis fungoides
* History of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML)
* Cerebral/meningeal disease related to the underlying malignancy

locations.facility: Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center - Basking Ridge

primary_outcomes.measure: Progression-free Survival Per Independent Review Facility (IRF)
primary_outcomes.description: The time from the date of randomization to the date of first documentation of progressive disease (PD), death due to any cause, or receipt of subsequent anticancer chemotherapy to treat residual or progressive disease whichever occurred first.
